title: Structured Retrieval, Knowledge Graphs, GraphRAG, and Multi-Hop Evidence · Transfer Build
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers structured retrieval, knowledge graphs, graphrag, and multi-hop evidence into a new provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of provenance-preserving multi-hop retriever with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Choose relational, document, graph, lexical, vector, or combined retrieval from question structure, source authority, joins, constraints, and evidence needs.
Use validated schemas, parameterized queries, authorization, row provenance, aggregation grain, and textual evidence when questions require structured facts.
Represent entities, relations, qualifiers, time, source spans, confidence, conflicts, schema, and extraction version and distinguish asserted from generated edges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Structured Retrieval Selection
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 3702 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to select structured, SQL, knowledge-graph, or GraphRAG retrieval only when multi-hop evidence beats baseline; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose relational, document, graph, lexical, vector, or combined retrieval from question structure, source authority, joins, constraints, and evidence needs.

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Multihop Path Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-multihop-path-evidence-j3b9kz" that uses case 3662 to select structured, SQL, knowledge-graph, or GraphRAG retrieval only when multi-hop evidence beats baseline. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Multihop Path Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Multihop Path Evidence into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Multihop Path Evidence, keep Rag Multihop Path Evidence connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-multihop-path-evidence-j3b9kz
# Competency: rag-multihop-path-evidence.
# Case 3662: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def evidence_rag_multihop_path_evidence_j3b9kz(entities: int = 12, relations: int = 18, hops: int = 2, provenance_edges: int = 18, schema_valid: bool = True, baseline_beaten: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_j3b9kz = "j3b9kz-zk9b3j-3662"
    assert evidence_variant_j3b9kz
    evidence_axes_j3b9kz = (
        "corpusj3b9kz",
        "queryzk9b3j",
        "tenant3662",
        "indexj3b9kz3662",
        "promptzk9b3j3662",
        "evaluation3662j3b9kz",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_j3b9kz)) == 6
    if entities <= 0 or relations < 0:
        return False, 0, "graph-shape"
    if hops < 1 or hops > 4:
        return False, hops, "hop-budget"
    if provenance_edges < relations:
        return False, provenance_edges, "edge-provenance"
    if not schema_valid:
        return False, relations, "structured-contract"
    if not baseline_beaten:
        return False, relations, "complexity-not-earned"
    assert provenance_edges >= relations
    return True, hops, "grounded-multihop"
